title: Continue splitting human and JSON CLI renderers

events.text: Separate CLI rendering from command logic. Existing human output must stay compatible; JSON mode must write JSON only to stdout.
Command handlers now return result objects and the text renderer reproduces existing snapshots.
The JSON snapshot contains ANSI color codes and one diagnostic line before the JSON document.
A global no-color flag fixed the snapshot but broke human color output, so that change was reverted.
Renderers own formatting. Diagnostics go to stderr; do not special-case commands.
src/cli/commands.ts and src/cli/text-renderer.ts are modified; json-renderer.ts exists but has no diagnostic routing test.

criticalConstraints.text: JSON mode writes JSON only to stdout and diagnostics to stderr.

objectiveAndState.text: Separate command results from rendering; text mode passes while JSON diagnostic routing is incomplete.

decisionsAndFailedAttempts.text: A global no-color flag was reverted because it broke human output.
Formatting belongs in renderers, with no command-specific branches.

completedAndPending.text: Result-returning handlers and text snapshots are complete; JSON stderr routing still needs a test and fix.

workspaceEvidence.text: commands.ts is modified and both renderer files are new.

nextAction.text: Add the JSON diagnostic-routing test, then remove ANSI and diagnostic text from stdout in the JSON renderer path.
